title: PHP Tags and Syntax
description: Learn about PHP opening/closing tags and basic syntax rules.

slides.title: PHP Tags
slides.content:
# How PHP is Embedded PHP code is embedded in HTML using special tags. The most common is ``. ```php

My Page

``` Everything outside the tags is sent directly to the browser as HTML.

slides.title: Short Tags (Not Recommended)
slides.content:
# Short Open Tags

PHP also supports short tags: `<? ... ?>` and `<?= ... ?>` (short echo).

*   `<?= $variable ?>` is equivalent to `<?php echo $variable; ?>`.
*   Short tags are not always enabled on servers, so it's safer to always use `<?php` and `<?=` (which is always available from PHP 5.4 onward).

**Best Practice:** Use `<?php` for code blocks and `<?=` for simple output.

slides.title: Statements and Semicolons
slides.content:
# Semicolons Are Important

In PHP, each instruction (statement) must end with a semicolon `;`.

```php
<?php
echo "Hello";   // correct
echo "World"    // error: missing semicolon
?>
```

The closing tag `?>` can optionally be omitted if the file contains only PHP code (no HTML). This is common to avoid accidental whitespace output.

quizData.question: Which of the following is the recommended way to output a variable in PHP?
quizData.explanation: `<?php echo $var; ?>` is the full form; `<?= $var ?>` is a convenient shorthand that is always available in modern PHP.
